step.title: Generate HTTP Middleware goal (conditional, phase=middleware)
step.rule: Fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs.
This step is CONDITIONAL — if no middleware was selected during discovery, skip entirely with a log message and proceed to the next step.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Check middleware selection in cross-cutting.md
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md. Find the middleware section (written by discover.xml Step 4.2/6.4 — covers CORS, request logging, rate limiting).
Extract which middleware features were selected/enabled. Build SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE list.

branch.condition: SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE is empty — no middleware selected
branch.action: Print: "No HTTP middleware selected — skipping Step 3.22"
SKIP — proceed to next step. Do NOT call goal-create.

substep.title: Resolve dependency goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=fixtures — store its ID as FIXTURES_GOAL_ID.

check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate middleware goal: no fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) found in goals.yaml — ensure fixtures step ran first"

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (MW-01..MW-04)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array from docs/task-plan-spec.md:725-737, filtering MW-01..MW-03 based on SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE:

criteria.item: CORS configured correctly for specified origins — Playwright E2E: OPTIONS request returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin header
Request logging captures method, path, status code, duration — read log file after test request to verify entry
Rate limiting returns 429 after configured threshold — integration test sends N+1 requests and asserts 429
All middleware registered in correct priority order in Symfony kernel — verify via bin/console debug:event-dispatcher or config inspection

substep.rule: Include MW-01, MW-02, MW-03 only when their corresponding middleware feature appears in SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE. MW-04 is always included (minimum 2 criteria).

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array based on selected middleware:
substep.rule: $BASE_URL references the value from docs/architecture/test-environment.md — never hardcoded (GM-05).

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: HTTP middleware goal: configure and test selected middleware features ({{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}}). See docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md for middleware selection decisions and configuration details. Middleware registration order matters — CORS before rate limiting before logging is typical Symfony priority ordering. All middleware registered via Symfony kernel event subscribers or framework config (e.g. nelmio/cors-bundle for CORS). Middleware files live in projects/{app}/src/Http/Middleware/ (cross-cutting, not per-BC).

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Kernel registration
investigator.pass: All selected middleware appear as listeners with correct priority ordering
investigator.fail: Missing middleware listener or incorrect priority

investigator.name: Integration tests
investigator.pass: Exit 0, all middleware tests green — CORS headers, log entries, rate limit 429 verified
investigator.fail: Test failure or missing test coverage for selected middleware features

investigator.name: Quality gates
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse projects/{app}/src/Http/Middleware/ --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check projects/{app}/src/Http/Middleware/
investigator.pass: PHPStan and ECS both exit 0 — zero errors
investigator.fail: Type errors or code style violations in middleware files

section.rule: 3 investigators (GM-08 allows 2-4). Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.value: Domain logic, application handlers, authentication, controller actions, database persistence, fixtures, E2E tests for non-middleware features — all handled by prior goals. Middleware features not selected during discovery are explicitly excluded.

substep.title: Inject code rules (apply [CODE-RULE-INJECTION])
substep.action: Bind RULE_FILES = the comma-joined middleware deliverable paths (projects/{app}/src/Http/Middleware/** — the middleware listeners/subscribers and their tests). Bind RULE_PHASE = `middleware` (this goal's phase token — pass it VERBATIM; do NOT remap it).

Run `tmux-cli rules match --files <RULE_FILES> --phase <RULE_PHASE> --json` and apply the spine's `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` procedure (<conventions> / <rule-to-goal-injection>): copy each NON-EMPTY `acceptance_line` into the ACCEPTANCE array (3.22.3); copy each `automated`/`mixed` `validate_cmd` into the VALIDATE array (3.22.4); fold `review` `validate_cmd`s into ONE convention-audit investigator (ADD; base is 3 from 3.22.5, so the 4th slot is free — no drop); add the `Code rules: <ids> — read <paths>` line to CONTEXT (3.22.5). Empty `rules` ⇒ inject nothing.
substep.rule: The phase token `middleware` is NOT in the Go `allowedPhases` enum — `rules match` returns an empty set, so this goal UNDER-injects (fail-safe: under-enforce, never wrong-enforce — mechanical parity, exactly as 3.21.7b documents for `error_handling`). Do NOT remap the phase token to a real phase to force a match. PRESERVE the 3 existing investigators.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Configure HTTP middleware: {{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}} with kernel registration and tests (≤120 chars — truncate list if needed)
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.22.3 (2-4 items depending on selections)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.22.5's 3 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.

substep.rule: Description dynamically lists selected middleware names — must stay ≤120 chars (MaxGoalDescriptionLength). If list exceeds limit, abbreviate: "Configure HTTP middleware: N features with kernel registration".
max_retries=3 — middleware goals have focused cross-cutting scope.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Middleware created: goal-{{ID}} (phase=middleware, depends_on=[{{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}}]) with 3 investigators, selected: {{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}}, max_retries=3"
